hints.to fall: That would be the infinitive of 'cadere'
hints.to err: That would be the infinitive of 'errare'
hints.to suffer: That would be the infinitive of 'pati'

hints.erro, errare, erravi, erratum: These are the principal parts of 'erro' (to wander/err)
hints.cado, cadere, cecidi, casum: These are the principal parts of 'cado' (to fall)
hints.fallo, fallere, fefelli, falsum: These are the principal parts of 'fallo' (to deceive)

hints.ablative: 'Domino' can be ablative, but here it indicates the one sinned against (dative)

hints.1st declension: 1st declension nouns typically end in -a in the nominative
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension dative singular would end in -i
hints.4th declension: 4th declension dative singular would end in -ui

translation: I have sinned against the Lord
